segments.text: There is a temple in India built
completely upside down. It's called Rani
Wow. And it's not just a religious site.
It's an engineering masterpiece that
saved the entire kingdom. Here to see
the god, you have to descend seven
levels deep into the earth. But why
built a temple in a deep dark hole?
Because in this drawridden land, the
most precious resource was not gold. It
was water. This temple was actually a
massive step well. The deeper you went,
the closer you got to the life-saving
groundwater that kept the entire city
alive during the hottest summer. But
here's the problem. Whenever people find
public sources of water, they tried to
pollute it. The queen's genius idea was
in the branding. She covered the walls
with sculptures of Lord Vishnu. Turning
a well into a divine space. By making
the water sacred, she ensured that the
people would not just use it, [music]
they would protect it, and they did.
Design so well thought. Subscribe for
more.
